generated_comment:
A: In our house also, we've kept it practical only yaar. My wife is far better at managing the kitchen, kids' routine, all these daily details, and I handle finances, bills, driver, outside work.
B: Fair enough.
A: Exactly. It's not about male-female, jo jahan better hai wohi sambhale. If I make breakfast once in a while, everyone acts like I'm some modern husband.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it naturalizes a gendered household division as mere practicality while assigning women domestic and childcare work and men finances/external tasks. It also encodes the 'modern husband' trope by treating minimal male participation in housework as exceptional.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
